title: artemisia furcata subsp. insulana
intro: Artemisia furcata subsp. insulana, also known as the Corsican wormwood, is a subspecies of the Artemisia furcata plant, a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Corsican wormwood include relief from digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and skin problems, as well as its use as a natural insect repellent. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and can also be used in herbal infusions and potpourri.
